excerpt: Allāh does not accept the testimony of a witness devoid of knowledge
source_location_hint: epistemological foundations of faith

excerpt: matters beyond sensory reach can be comprehended through reason, provided one possesses a teacher who explains them and the will to understand
source_location_hint: doctrine of twin paths to knowledge

excerpt: Shirk in my community is more concealed than the crawling of a black ant upon a black stone in the darkness of night
source_work: Sunan Ibn Majah and Ismaili exegetical tradition
source_author: attributed to Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him)
source_authority: hadith tradition, cited in Ismaili interpretive sources
source_location_hint: hidden polytheism doctrine

excerpt: though human in outward form and appearance, the two groups possess no correspondence in spiritual and psychological reality
source_location_hint: humanity and spiritual states

excerpt: wilāyat of the Wasī is as obligatory as obedience to the Messenger of Allāh
source_work: al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama (Lamps in Proof of the Imamate)
source_location_hint: doctrine of necessary guardianship

excerpt: the meanings of Qur'ānic verses have no limit, like a boundless sea into which every seeker must dive
source_work: Taʾwil al-Daʿaʾim and transmitted sayings of Imam Jaʿfar al-Ṣādiq
source_location_hint: inexhaustibility of Qur'ānic meaning

excerpt: opened a thousand chapters of knowledge from every chapter a thousand more
source_location_hint: Quranic exegesis on 2:24

excerpt: produce other comprehensive attributes like this and knowledge of the universe
source_location_hint: Quranic commentary on spiritual challenge

excerpt: I have been sent to all mankind, to the fair-skinned and dark-skinned alike
source_location_hint: General prophetic tradition

excerpt: Whoever has an outward miracle possesses an inner miracle of immeasurable greatness
source_location_hint: Esoteric Quranic interpretation

excerpt: I have attained certainty such that were all veils removed my faith would not increase; I possess awareness of every particle through knowledge from the Messenger
source_author: Amir al-Muminin Ali ibn Abi Talib
source_location_hint: Ali's claim to comprehensive knowledge

excerpt: O Ali, on account of your knowledge two parties have perished: one deprives you of rank, the other elevates you beyond it
source_location_hint: Tradition on Ali and the two extremes

excerpt: Al-Hasan and al-Husayn are from me and I am from them
source_location_hint: Prophetic statement on grandsons

excerpt: O Ali, your flesh is my flesh and your blood is my blood; your station with me is as the soul in the body
source_location_hint: Prophetic statement on Ali's rank

excerpt: Both the Ahl al-Raʾy and the Ahl al-Hadith are compelled by reason to acknowledge taʾwīl in certain verses where the apparent meaning contradicts reason.
source_location_hint: Chapter on esoteric interpretation

excerpt: The firmly grounded in knowledge confirm what they themselves understand—they are witnesses, not blind assayers.
source_location_hint: Section on knowledge and belief

excerpt: Allah bears witness, and the angels, and those possessed of knowledge—establishing the knowledgeable as witnesses to divine truth.
source_location_hint: On divine attestation

excerpt: We shall show them Our signs in the horizons and in their own souls—the inward and outward mirrors of creation.
source_location_hint: On the correspondence of microcosm and macrocosm

excerpt: Allah has fashioned His religion after the pattern of His creation, that through creation you may judge His religion.
source_location_hint: On the analogy of creation and religion

excerpt: You are a mercy to the worlds—the Messenger extends mercy to all the worlds of which Allah is Lord.
source_location_hint: On the universal nature of mercy

excerpt: I am the point beneath the Bāʾ—Amīr al-Muʾminīn on the distinction between Makka and Bakkah.
source_location_hint: Sermon on esoteric knowledge

excerpt: The example of ʿAlī is the example of the Kaʿba—what is understood of one must be understood of the other.
source_location_hint: On the correspondence of the Prophet and Imām

excerpt: I am the point of the worlds—ʿAlī's declaration of his exalted yet subordinate rank to the Prophet.
source_location_hint: On relative exaltation

excerpt: The infallible Imāms will not die until each establishes a successor like himself—the eternal chain of guardianship.
source_location_hint: On the perpetuation of the Imāmate

excerpt: We made the son of Mary and his mother a sign—the Imāms as signs and proofs of Allah in the world.
source_location_hint: On the Imāms as āyāt

excerpt: Those arrogant will not enter Paradise until the camel passes through the needle's eye—a parable of spiritual transformation.
source_location_hint: On spiritual humility and the esoteric meaning of the parable

excerpt: The breadth of Paradise equals the breadth of the heavens and earth—its vastness encompasses all creation.
source_location_hint: On the measure of Paradise

excerpt: One should not pray when a camel is before you, for in every camel dwells a devil—the camel as symbol of arrogance.
source_location_hint: Interpretation of the camel symbol

excerpt: O children of Adam, let not Satan deceive you as he drove your parents from Paradise, stripping them of their raiment—the garment of piety as the soul's protection.
source_location_hint: On the garment of monotheism

excerpt: The garment of piety is best—the raiment of true monotheism adorns the believer for Paradise.
source_location_hint: On spiritual adornment

excerpt: The king descended from his throne and sat humbly before the preacher, understanding the needle's eye as the passage through humiliation—the narrative frame for spiritual transformation.
source_location_hint: The parable of the king and preacher, Twenty-second Session

excerpt: You see them looking at you with their eyes, yet they see not—the distinction between outward gaze and inward sight.
source_location_hint: On the levels of perception

excerpt: When We intend a thing, We say to it: 'Be,' and it is—the creative utterance that transforms potentiality into actuality.
source_location_hint: On the nature of creation and manifestation

excerpt: I and ʿAlī are from the same light—the Prophet's declaration of the unity of essence between himself and his successor.
source_location_hint: On the light of the Prophet and Imām

excerpt: Whomsoever I am master of, ʿAlī is master of—the proclamation at Ghadīr Khumm establishing ʿAlī's succession.
source_location_hint: On the appointment of the successor

excerpt: Did not the disbelievers perceive when We separated the station of the guardian from that of prophethood?—the historical actualization of the spiritual principle.
source_location_hint: Final interpretation of the heavens and earth

excerpt: Everything whose existence is eternal and fixed is the same and that is God...the religion of Matin through which we have attained Tawheed. This religion has been present in all times and has been brought by all the prophets one after the other.
source_location_hint: Section 22: La ilaha illallahu exegesis

excerpt: Islam is the cause of Allah through which we have come to know Allah...the Messenger's deputy and the true Caliph will also be called the Cause of Allah, who will stand up with guidance.
source_work: KASHKOLE; paralleled in Kirmani's al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama
source_location_hint: On causality and the Imam's mediatory role

excerpt: Man, being the noblest of creatures, deserved the blessing that He made him know His face...blessed with saints, prophets, angels, the Throne, the Chair, Paradise and all the spiritual realms.
source_work: KASHKOLE; resonant with Nasir-i Khusraw's Jamiʿ al-Hikmatayn
source_location_hint: On human nobility and divine knowledge

excerpt: Each rank in the summons has a circle within which he executes his duties. It is impermissible for a person to overstep the authority of his station...the system of the invitation will suffer disruption.
source_work: KASHKOLE; attributed to Mullā Saʿd Allāh ibn Hazrat Nasrallāh
source_author: Nasir-i Khusraw tradition or Mulla Sadullah
source_location_hint: Section 23: Wa kull fi falak exegesis; orbital hierarchy of daʿwa

excerpt: This verse alludes to the very love that the Prophet proclaimed at Ghadīr Khumm concerning ʿAlī...In this verse, Allah addresses the Prophet: 'Announce to the people, I do not ask of you any recompense for my prophethood except that you love my family.'
source_work: KASHKOLE; paralleled in al-Muʾayyad al-Shirazi's al-Majalis al-Muʾayyadiyya and Kirmani's al-Masabih
source_location_hint: Section 24: Qul la as'alukum exegesis; Ghadir revelation

excerpt: Allah created this vast universe out of love for these five [Panj-Akh], declaring: 'I have not raised the vault of the heavens, nor laid the floor of the earth...except in love for the five pure ones clothed in the mantle and purified from all defilement.'
source_work: KASHKOLE; resonant with Al-Muʾayyad's Diwan and Qadi al-Nuʿman's Daʿaʾim al-Islam
source_location_hint: Theology of creation and Ahl al-Bayt; Fatimid cosmology

excerpt: The Prophet declared: 'Adorn your gatherings with the remembrance of ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib, for the remembrance of ʿAlī is my remembrance, and my remembrance is the remembrance of Allah, and the remembrance of Allah is worship.'
source_work: KASHKOLE; paralleled in al-Sijistani's al-Iftikhar and Qadi al-Nuʿman's Asas al-Taʾwil
source_location_hint: Hadith on dhikr and devotion to ʿAlī in Ismaili tradition

excerpt: Gabriel came with a warning from Allah to the Prophet: 'If you associate anyone with ʿAlī in the inward invitation, Allah will render all your deeds null.'
source_work: KASHKOLE; paralleled in Kirmani's al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama and Qadi al-Nuʿman's Taʾwil al-Daʾaʾim
source_location_hint: Section 25: Taʾwil of 48:2; exclusivity of ʿAlī's wilāya

excerpt: When he reached the station of Ghadīr Khumm, Allah commanded him with great severity: 'O Messenger! Convey what has been revealed to you from your Lord. If you do not, you have not conveyed His message.' The verse that truly stands out: 'Convey what has been revealed to you concerning ʿAlī from your Lord.'
source_work: KASHKOLE; paralleled in al-Muʾayyad's al-Majalis and Kirmani's Tanbih al-Hadi
source_location_hint: Section 25: Ghadīr revelation and taʾwīl of 5:67

excerpt: Syednā Ghulām Ḥusayn Ṣāḥib used to say that the Sultan of the Gnostics has proclaimed: 'Whoever knows himself knows his Lord.' One supposes the self to be singular, yet upon reflection, we discover three within it. The lowest degree is the vegetative soul, next the sensitive soul, and above all the rational soul.
source_location_hint: Section 26: Taʾwīl of 50:16; metaphysics of the soul and divine nearness

excerpt: the Qur'an, embodied as it is in letters and words, exhibits profound variations
source_location_hint: sermon on Qur'anic interpretation

excerpt: one verse establishes a conception in the mind, yet shortly thereafter another verse compels imagination toward an opposite understanding
source_location_hint: on apparent contradictions in Qur'anic verses

excerpt: one of our preachers lifted the Qur'an above his head and exclaimed, By Allah! This Book has led astray generation after generation
source_location_hint: discourse on Qur'anic exegesis

excerpt: the Ahl al-Bayt are the soul of the Ahl al-Bayt who unveil the hidden meaning beneath the words; should their soul be separated from the Qur'an, it becomes lifeless
source_location_hint: on the Qur'an and the guidance of the Ahl al-Bayt

excerpt: Allah swears by the Messenger of Allah regarding obedience to ʿAlī, and it is evident that the oath is taken by the mightiest things
source_location_hint: on divine oaths and their meanings

excerpt: the Messenger shall never be satisfied, and therefore the process of Allah's giving shall never cease
source_location_hint: on the verse about the Prophet's satisfaction

excerpt: the glory of the Qāʾim, who emerges at the end of this seventh cycle, shall integrate all the forms of unveiling and the cycles that came before, which the Qur'an designates as the Mīqāt
source_location_hint: on the cycles of prophecy and the role of the Qāʾim

excerpt: the righteous and the God-fearing are those who enter the house through the door, not by tearing through the wall—the one who acknowledges the wilāya of ʿAlī speaks truth
source_location_hint: esoteric exegesis on entering the house through proper doors

excerpt: He created the universe in six days, then sat upon His Throne
source_location_hint: on the creation in six days and divine rest

excerpt: Adam came from above the human dimension; Noah from the left; Abraham from behind; Moses from below; Jesus from the right; and Muḥammad al-Muṣṭafā from the front—each prophet coming from the six aspects of humanity
source_location_hint: on the six messengers and their correspondence to the dimensions of man
